Documentation on finding, using, and building Flask extensions to add functionality to applications.

# Purpose
Extensions in a Flask application are additional packages that enhance the application's functionality. These extensions can provide features such as email support, database connectivity, or even entire frameworks for building specific types of applications like REST APIs. Flask extensions are typically named with a pattern like "Flask-Foo" or "Foo-Flask" and can be found on PyPI by searching for packages tagged with `Framework :: Flask`. To use an extension, refer to its documentation for installation and configuration details. Extensions usually obtain their configuration from `app.config` and require an application instance during initialization. If existing extensions do not meet specific needs, developers can create and publish their own extensions by following guidelines in the Flask extension development documentation.
